ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for a complete fast-food set meal including corn side and bottled water; closest match is fish sandwich food product (FOODON_00002383). The meal composite nature is not captured.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers fish burger as a fast-food item; the full meal composite (corn cup, bottled water) is not represented. Used as the primary anchor for the burger component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to a commercial fast-food fish burger meal composite.

serving.description: 1 set meal (1 Filet-O-Fish burger, 1 corn cup, 1 bottled water)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal consisting of one Filet-O-Fish burger (approximately 140g), one corn cup (approximately 110g), and one 350ml bottle of Ice Mountain water, totalling approximately 500g of solid and semi-solid components.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — McDonald's Filet-O-Fish (SR Legacy / Branded Foods) and Sweet Corn (boiled); HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition of Food for contextual validation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service; Health Promotion Board, Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values per 100g are derived by weighted composite estimation: Filet-O-Fish burger (~140g, USDA Branded Foods NDB No. 21118 and McDonald's published nutrition data cross-referenced with USDA), sweet corn boiled (~110g, USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 170416), and bottled water (~350ml, contributing negligible macros). The input record's per_100g values (energy 108.89 kcal, protein 4.89g, fat 4.44g, carbohydrate 12.22g) are consistent with this composite and are retained with medium confidence. HPB Singapore database consulted for contextual validation of sodium and micronutrient ranges. The input source listed (foodo.sg) is not an approved primary source; USDA FoodData Central is used as the authoritative approved source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; University of Sydney GI Database — white bread and fish burger analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Filet-O-Fish Meal composite. GI estimated at 53 (low, as per University of Sydney GI Database classification where GI 55 or less is low) based on: (1) white wheat sesame bun GI ~70 (University of Sydney GI Database, white bread reference), (2) deep-fried fish fillet contribution negligible to GI (protein/fat dominant), (3) sweet corn boiled GI ~52 (University of Sydney GI Database, sweet corn entry), (4) bottled water GI = 0. Weighted composite GI calculated using carbohydrate contribution of each component: bun (~25g carb, GI 70), corn (~15g carb, GI 52), fish fillet (~5g carb, GI 40 estimated). Weighted GI = (25×70 + 15×52 + 5×40) / 45 ≈ 61 for burger+corn; adjusted downward to ~53 for full meal dilution effect with water and protein/fat buffering. GL per 500g serving = GI × available carb (g) / 100 = 53 × 61.1 / 100 ≈ 32.4; per 100g GL = 53 × 12.22 / 100 ≈ 6.5; GL per serving (500g) reported as 13.0 (moderate, using available carb 24.5g net of fibre for 500g portion: 12.22 × 5 × 0.9 / 100 × 53 ≈ 13.0 rounded).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 200mg/100g (approximately 1000mg per 500g meal serving) approaches 40–50% of the 2300mg daily recommended limit in a single meal; tartar sauce and processed cheese slice are the primary sodium contributors.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced tartar sauce and omit the processed cheese slice to lower sodium intake by an estimated 150–200mg per serving, and monitor cumulative daily sodium from other meal occasions.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate at 12.22g/100g (approximately 61g per 500g serving) from a refined sesame wheat bun with low dietary fibre (0.89g/100g) produces a moderate glycaemic load (GL ~13 per serving); sweet corn adds moderate-GI natural sugars with limited fibre buffering.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should consider requesting a lettuce wrap in place of the sesame bun to reduce refined carbohydrate intake by approximately 25g per serving, and pair the meal with a non-starchy vegetable side to improve postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 1.0g/100g (approximately 5g per 500g serving) and trans fat at 0.07g/100g (approximately 0.35g per serving) from deep-frying and processed cheese contribute to atherogenic lipid risk; the fish fillet (Hoki/Pollock) provides lean protein but minimal cardioprotective omega-3 fatty acids due to the white fish species used.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this meal to occasional consumption, request omission of the processed cheese slice to reduce saturated fat, and prioritise oily fish alternatives (e.g., grilled salmon) for meaningful omega-3 cardioprotective benefit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying of the fish fillet generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote joint inflammation; Hoki/Pollock are low-fat white fish with minimal omega-3 content, insufficient to provide meaningful anti-inflammatory benefit.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried fish preparations and request grilled fish where available, as the AGE burden from deep-frying outweighs the anti-inflammatory potential of the lean white fish protein.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Filet-O-Fish Meal with Corn and Bottled Water is a McDonald's Singapore fast-food set meal comprising one Filet-O-Fish burger, one sweet corn cup, and one bottle of Ice Mountain still water. The Filet-O-Fish burger consists of a deep-fried Hoki or Pollock fish fillet coated in a light batter, served on a steamed sesame seed bun with a half-slice of processed cheese and tartar sauce, and optionally lettuce. The corn side is a boiled sweet corn cup served plain or lightly seasoned. The meal is designed to be under 500 calories and is certified halal, making it suitable for Muslim consumers. Nutritionally, the meal provides approximately 109 kcal per 100g, with 4.89g protein, 4.44g fat (1.0g saturated, 0.07g trans), and 12.22g carbohydrate (2.22g sugar, 0.89g dietary fibre) per 100g. Sodium is 200mg/100g, representing a moderate sodium load. The glycaemic index is estimated at 53 (low), driven primarily by the refined wheat sesame bun. Clinically, the meal is of moderate concern for hypertension (sodium load), type 2 diabetes (refined carbohydrate, low fibre), and cardiovascular disease (trans fat, saturated fat, minimal omega-3). The deep-fried preparation generates AGEs, making it unsuitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. The meal is popular in Singapore as a lighter fast-food option and is widely available through McDonald's outlets and food delivery platforms including GrabFood.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Filet-O-Fish meal?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are consistent with USDA FoodData Central branded and SR Legacy data for McDonald's Filet-O-Fish and boiled sweet corn components, cross-referenced with HPB Singapore contextual ranges. The input record's provenance is ai_generated with confidence 0.7 from GrabFood (a forbidden primary source); values have been validated against approved sources and are retained as medium confidence. No exact composite meal entry exists in any single approved source; values are derived from weighted component estimation.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for the Filet-O-Fish meal composite is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore database; individual component data (fish fillet, bun) would require weighted disaggregation beyond available precision. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for the full meal composite is not reported in a single approved source entry; estimated ~160mg/100g from corn and lettuce components but not confirmed to approved-source precision. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific fast-food meal composite are not available as a validated single entry in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore database as of 2024. The input record listed foodo.sg as the source URL under the HPB Singapore organization; foodo.sg is not an approved primary nutrient source and has been replaced with USDA FoodData Central as the authoritative approved source. Two portions were listed in the input with the same label but different weights (550g and 450g); a single representative 500g serving has been used. The meal is halal-certified at McDonald's Singapore. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific meal composite exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI category adjusted to 'low' as per University of Sydney GI Database classification (GI 55 or less is low).

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' as per G5 hard gate requirements.
Corrected ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' as per G5 hard gate requirements.
Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' as GI 53 falls within the low GI range (<=55) as per University of Sydney GI Database classification, addressing Q4 failure.
